verses.latin_vulgate: Custodi pedem tuum ingrediens domum Dei,  et appropinqua ut audias.  Multo enim melior est obedientia quam stultorum victimæ,  qui nesciunt quid faciunt mali.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁמֹ֣ר (רגליך) [רַגְלְךָ֗] כַּאֲשֶׁ֤ר תֵּלֵךְ֙ אֶל־בֵּ֣ית הָאֱלֹהִ֔ים וְקָר֣וֹב לִשְׁמֹ֔עַ מִתֵּ֥ת הַכְּסִילִ֖ים זָ֑בַח כִּֽי־אֵינָ֥ם יוֹדְעִ֖ים לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת רָֽע׃
verses.king_james: Keep thy foot when thou goest to the house of God, and be more ready to hear, than to give the sacrifice of fools: for they consider not that they do evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne temere quid loquaris,  neque cor tuum sit velox ad proferendum sermonem coram Deo.  Deus enim in cælo, et tu super terram ;  idcirco sint pauci sermones tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תְּבַהֵ֨ל עַל־פִּ֜יךָ וְלִבְּךָ֧ אַל־יְמַהֵ֛ר לְהוֹצִ֥יא דָבָ֖ר לִפְנֵ֣י הָאֱלֹהִ֑ים כִּ֣י הָאֱלֹהִ֤ים בַּשָּׁמַ֙יִם֙ וְאַתָּ֣ה עַל־הָאָ֔רֶץ עַל־כֵּ֛ן יִהְי֥וּ דְבָרֶ֖יךָ מְעַטִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: Be not rash with thy mouth, and let not thine heart be hasty to utter any thing before God: for God is in heaven, and thou upon earth: therefore let thy words be few.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multas curas sequuntur somnia,  et in multis sermonibus invenietur stultitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֛י בָּ֥א הַחֲל֖וֹם בְּרֹ֣ב עִנְיָ֑ן וְק֥וֹל כְּסִ֖יל בְּרֹ֥ב דְּבָרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: For a dream cometh through the multitude of business; and a fool’s voice is known by multitude of words.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quid vovisti Deo,  ne moreris reddere :  displicet enim ei infidelis et stulta promissio,  sed quodcumque voveris redde :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּאֲשֶׁר֩ תִּדֹּ֨ר נֶ֜דֶר לֵֽאלֹהִ֗ים אַל־תְּאַחֵר֙ לְשַׁלְּמ֔וֹ כִּ֛י אֵ֥ין חֵ֖פֶץ בַּכְּסִילִ֑ים אֵ֥ת אֲשֶׁר־תִּדֹּ֖ר שַׁלֵּֽם׃
verses.king_james: When thou vowest a vow unto God, defer not to pay it; for he hath no pleasure in fools: pay that which thou hast vowed.

verses.latin_vulgate: multoque melius est non vovere,  quam post votum promissa non reddere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֖וֹב אֲשֶׁ֣ר לֹֽא־תִדֹּ֑ר מִשֶּׁתִּדּ֖וֹר וְלֹ֥א תְשַׁלֵּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Better is it that thou shouldest not vow, than that thou shouldest vow and not pay.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne dederis os tuum ut peccare facias carnem tuam,  neque dicas coram angelo :  Non est providentia :  ne forte iratus Deus contra sermones tuos  dissipet cuncta opera manuum tuarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּתֵּ֤ן אֶת־פִּ֙יךָ֙ לַחֲטִ֣יא אֶת־בְּשָׂרֶ֔ךָ וְאַל־תֹּאמַר֙ לִפְנֵ֣י הַמַּלְאָ֔ךְ כִּ֥י שְׁגָגָ֖ה הִ֑יא לָ֣מָּה יִקְצֹ֤ף הָֽאֱלֹהִים֙ עַל־קוֹלֶ֔ךָ וְחִבֵּ֖ל אֶת־מַעֲשֵׂ֥ה יָדֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Suffer not thy mouth to cause thy flesh to sin; neither say thou before the angel, that it was an error: wherefore should God be angry at thy voice, and destroy the work of thine hands?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi multa sunt somnia,  plurimæ sunt vanitates, et sermones innumeri ;  tu vero Deum time.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י בְרֹ֤ב חֲלֹמוֹת֙ וַהֲבָלִ֔ים וּדְבָרִ֖ים הַרְבֵּ֑ה כִּ֥י אֶת־הָאֱלֹהִ֖ים יְרָֽא׃
verses.king_james: For in the multitude of dreams and many words there are also divers vanities: but fear thou God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si videris calumnias egenorum, et violenta judicia,  et subverti justitiam in provincia,  non mireris super hoc negotio :  quia excelso excelsior est alius,  et super hos quoque eminentiores sunt alii ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־עֹ֣שֶׁק רָ֠שׁ וְגֵ֨זֶל מִשְׁפָּ֤ט וָצֶ֙דֶק֙ תִּרְאֶ֣ה בַמְּדִינָ֔ה אַל־תִּתְמַ֖הּ עַל־הַחֵ֑פֶץ כִּ֣י גָבֹ֜הַּ מֵעַ֤ל גָּבֹ֙הַּ֙ שֹׁמֵ֔ר וּגְבֹהִ֖ים עֲלֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: If thou seest the oppression of the poor, and violent perverting of judgment and justice in a province, marvel not at the matter: for he that is higher than the highest regardeth; and there be higher than they.

verses.latin_vulgate: et insuper universæ terræ rex imperat servienti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיִתְר֥וֹן אֶ֖רֶץ בַּכֹּ֣ל (היא) [ה֑וּא] מֶ֥לֶךְ לְשָׂדֶ֖ה נֶעֱבָֽד׃
verses.king_james: Moreover the profit of the earth is for all: the king himself is served by the field.

verses.latin_vulgate: Avarus non implebitur pecunia,  et qui amat divitias fructum non capiet ex eis ;  et hoc ergo vanitas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֹהֵ֥ב כֶּ֙סֶף֙ לֹא־יִשְׂבַּ֣ע כֶּ֔סֶף וּמִֽי־אֹהֵ֥ב בֶּהָמ֖וֹן לֹ֣א תְבוּאָ֑ה גַּם־זֶ֖ה הָֽבֶל׃
verses.king_james: He that loveth silver shall not be satisfied with silver; nor he that loveth abundance with increase: this is also vanity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi multæ sunt opes,  multi et qui comedunt eas.  Et quid prodest possessori,  nisi quod cernit divitias oculis suis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּרְבוֹת֙ הַטּוֹבָ֔ה רַבּ֖וּ אוֹכְלֶ֑יהָ וּמַה־כִּשְׁרוֹן֙ לִבְעָלֶ֔יהָ כִּ֖י אִם־[רְא֥וּת] (ראית) עֵינָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: When goods increase, they are increased that eat them: and what good is there to the owners thereof, saving the beholding of them with their eyes?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dulcis est somnus operanti,  sive parum sive multum comedat ;  saturitas autem divitis non sinit eum dormire.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְתוּקָה֙ שְׁנַ֣ת הָעֹבֵ֔ד אִם־מְעַ֥ט וְאִם־הַרְבֵּ֖ה יֹאכֵ֑ל וְהַשָּׂבָע֙ לֶֽעָשִׁ֔יר אֵינֶ֛נּוּ מַנִּ֥יחַֽ ל֖וֹ לִישֽׁוֹן׃
verses.king_james: The sleep of a labouring man is sweet, whether he eat little or much: but the abundance of the rich will not suffer him to sleep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Est et alia infirmitas pessima quam vidi sub sole :  divitiæ conservatæ in malum domini sui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵ֚שׁ רָעָ֣ה חוֹלָ֔ה רָאִ֖יתִי תַּ֣חַת הַשָּׁ֑מֶשׁ עֹ֛שֶׁר שָׁמ֥וּר לִבְעָלָ֖יו לְרָעָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: There is a sore evil which I have seen under the sun, namely, riches kept for the owners thereof to their hurt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pereunt enim in afflictione pessima :  generavit filium qui in summa egestate erit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָבַ֛ד הָעֹ֥שֶׁר הַה֖וּא בְּעִנְיַ֣ן רָ֑ע וְהוֹלִ֣יד בֵּ֔ן וְאֵ֥ין בְּיָד֖וֹ מְאֽוּמָה׃
verses.king_james: But those riches perish by evil travail: and he begetteth a son, and there is nothing in his hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut egressus est nudus de utero matris suæ, sic revertetur,  et nihil auferet secum de labore suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּאֲשֶׁ֤ר יָצָא֙ מִבֶּ֣טֶן אִמּ֔וֹ עָר֛וֹם יָשׁ֥וּב לָלֶ֖כֶת כְּשֶׁבָּ֑א וּמְא֙וּמָה֙ לֹא־יִשָּׂ֣א בַעֲמָל֔וֹ שֶׁיֹּלֵ֖ךְ בְּיָדֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: As he came forth of his mother’s womb, naked shall he return to go as he came, and shall take nothing of his labour, which he may carry away in his hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Miserabilis prorsus infirmitas :  quomodo venit, sic revertetur.  Quid ergo prodest ei quod laboravit in ventum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגַם־זֹה֙ רָעָ֣ה חוֹלָ֔ה כׇּל־עֻמַּ֥ת שֶׁבָּ֖א כֵּ֣ן יֵלֵ֑ךְ וּמַה־יִּתְר֣וֹן ל֔וֹ שֶֽׁיַּעֲמֹ֖ל לָרֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: And this also is a sore evil, that in all points as he came, so shall he go: and what profit hath he that hath laboured for the wind?

verses.latin_vulgate: cunctis diebus vitæ suæ comedit in tenebris,  et in curis multis, et in ærumna atque tristitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֥ם כׇּל־יָמָ֖יו בַּחֹ֣שֶׁךְ יֹאכֵ֑ל וְכָעַ֥ס הַרְבֵּ֖ה וְחׇלְי֥וֹ וָקָֽצֶף׃
verses.king_james: All his days also he eateth in darkness, and he hath much sorrow and wrath with his sickness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc itaque visum est mihi bonum,  ut comedat quis et bibat,  et fruatur lætitia ex labore suo  quo laboravit ipse sub sole,  numero dierum vitæ suæ  quos dedit ei Deus ;  et hæc est pars illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֞ה אֲשֶׁר־רָאִ֣יתִי אָ֗נִי ט֣וֹב אֲשֶׁר־יָפֶ֣ה לֶֽאֱכוֹל־וְ֠לִשְׁתּ֠וֹת וְלִרְא֨וֹת טוֹבָ֜ה בְּכׇל־עֲמָל֣וֹ ׀ שֶׁיַּעֲמֹ֣ל תַּֽחַת־הַשֶּׁ֗מֶשׁ מִסְפַּ֧ר יְמֵי־חַיָּ֛ו אֲשֶׁר־נָֽתַן־ל֥וֹ הָאֱלֹהִ֖ים כִּי־ה֥וּא חֶלְקֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Behold that which I have seen: it is good and comely for one to eat and to drink, and to enjoy the good of all his labour that he taketh under the sun all the days of his life, which God giveth him: for it is his portion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omni homini cui dedit Deus divitias atque substantiam,  potestatemque ei tribuit ut comedat ex eis,  et fruatur parte sua, et lætetur de labore suo :  hoc est donum Dei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֣ם כׇּֽל־הָאָדָ֡ם אֲשֶׁ֣ר נָֽתַן־ל֣וֹ הָאֱלֹהִים֩ עֹ֨שֶׁר וּנְכָסִ֜ים וְהִשְׁלִיט֨וֹ לֶאֱכֹ֤ל מִמֶּ֙נּוּ֙ וְלָשֵׂ֣את אֶת־חֶלְק֔וֹ וְלִשְׂמֹ֖חַ בַּעֲמָל֑וֹ זֹ֕ה מַתַּ֥ת אֱלֹהִ֖ים הִֽיא׃
verses.king_james: Every man also to whom God hath given riches and wealth, and hath given him power to eat thereof, and to take his portion, and to rejoice in his labour; this is the gift of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim satis recordabitur dierum vitæ suæ,  eo quod Deus occupet deliciis cor ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֚י לֹ֣א הַרְבֵּ֔ה יִזְכֹּ֖ר אֶת־יְמֵ֣י חַיָּ֑יו כִּ֧י הָאֱלֹהִ֛ים מַעֲנֶ֖ה בְּשִׂמְחַ֥ת לִבּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: For he shall not much remember the days of his life; because God answereth him in the joy of his heart.
